channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Large black mats dumped on king & queen street, 23rd June
item.description:
Filthy large black mats left on street for many weeks (nearer the Browning St end of King & Queen Street)
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): King and Queen Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Driver mounting pavement & cycleway to bypass modal filter outside school, 23rd June
item.description:
A driver mounted the pavement and cycleway to bypass the modal filter and bollards (central in the cycleway) to travel at speed south along Comber Grove where it is pedestrianised outside the former primary school. This happened at 18:00 on Mon 22 June 2026, the driver travelling southbound and then into Blucher Road. This poses a threat to pedestrian and cyclist safety, which could be prevented by additional bollards.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Comber Grove, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Crap dumped in Brett close, 22nd June
item.description:
Load of crap dumped in Brett close, again!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brett Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping in doorway, 22nd June
item.description:
Cabinet and other items dumped in doorway of entrance to national grid stowage site. On Stowage, by the corner of Gonson St

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Stowage, Deptford
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: None of the lights in this corner work, 22nd June
item.description:
There is a long strip here where none of the lights work and it makes it unsafe to walk at night. There has been an increase in men hanging around this area too which I have noticed since the lights went out and it makes me feel very uncomfortable and unsafe to walk through
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Yarnton Way, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trees and growth from base need pruning urgently, 22nd June
item.description:
The tree foliage and growth at bottom of the trees is out of control on this road and encroaching onto the road between Park Crescent & Bexley Road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lesney Park, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wooden crate in Lamlash Garden, Hayles St end., 22nd June
item.description:
This was dumped in Lamlash some time ago. We do not need it so can it please be removed as it has become a bit of a dumping area. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 48, Elliott's Row, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Picnic area always dirty, 22nd June
item.description:
What is the point of this location? You basically have a picnic area right next to a river so some of the rubbish will inevitably end up in the river. It seems that the people that use it just dump stuff all over the place and then it is blown around.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ravensbourne Greenway, Southend, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Combermere rd covered in litter, 22nd June
item.description:
Combermere rd covered in litter

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Combermere Road, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Building waste dumped on Winchester rd., 22nd June
item.description:
Looks like building waste from a commercial kitchen. There is an industrial-sized air filter with cooking grease on it, a tub of heavy duty adhesive, metal panels, broken plaster sheets and a large blue crate. It was dumped sometime over the weekend.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Winchester Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Black bag of household waste in street, 22nd June
item.description:
Litter covering road and pavement from bag

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Combermere Road, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping by resident living at 13 Benhill Road, 22nd June
item.description:
This evening, I saw the male resident from 13 Benhill Road exit from his garden, use a shared pathway and go dump various items (definitely the two pieces of furniture) on Harris Street (just opposite 33 Harris Street). He had to made several trips back and forth to dump all his unwanted items.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Harris Street, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: AGAIN this street light is out, 22nd June
item.description:
Once a month this happens. Is there some sort of money saving thing going on? If so, we should be told about it. And why this light only?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Green Lane, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree blocking natural light, 22nd June
item.description:
Very over grown hanging into our garden we are constantly cleaning our garden. also blocked all the sunlight for us neighbours and has reached the balcony upstairs . There was also people who was homeless people taking shelter there. Not good at all

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Wimbourne Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A Fly-tip Medium - 4 to 9 black bags problem has been found, 22nd June
item.description:
A Fly-tip Medium - a Matress and sofa has been dumped in the middle of the estate

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Gorefield Place, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load (Estates)

item.title: Dumped items, 22nd June
item.description:
People keep throwing their rubbish near our property, this needs to be investigated and dealt with. Also the grass needs to be cut, it is long overdue… The gov gardeners have been twice over the few months just to cut the roadside edge and that’s it. Can the overgrown grass be cut and sorted, thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 111, Leesons Hill, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cars blocking fire access and driveway, 22nd June
item.description:
Fire access and emergency access blocked. Also 4 garages meaning cars blocked in.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kyverdale Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox cub on council green in a council wheelie bin., 22nd June
item.description:
This is the 3rd time of reporting this, there was a dead fox cub in my front garden, I removed it and placed it in a bin bag and then wrapped it in a white cotton bag because it had started to smell and had flies over it, I then placed it in an abandoned wheelie bin that has been on the green for many months (number 16a written on it) which is under the tree nearest the parking bays, for whatever reason my reports were removed and the fox cub is still in the bin and in this heat it absolutely smells and will only get worse, you can now smell it at a distance and it is not pleasant at all, I am a council tenant and the wheelie bin is on a council green, so cannot understand why it has not being collected or why both my reports were removed and ignored, can this be sorted ASAP.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sibthorpe Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 nitrous oxide canisters, 22nd June
item.description:
2 nitrous oxide containers. By the green comms box opposite the nhs clinic 14 The Crescent BR3 1DU.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, The Crescent, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 22nd June
item.description:
Blocking vehicle access to Thames Water Facility

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Battle Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
